item.title: This Week on The Analog Antiquarian 摘要
item.description:
This article is part of a series examining Shakespeare's Henry VI, Part 3, focusing on its historical context, themes, and literary significance. It explores the play's depiction of the Wars of the Roses, character dynamics, and how Shakespeare adapted historical events for dramatic effect. The analysis highlights key scenes, political intrigue, and the play's role in the broader Henriad cycle.
标签: Shakespeare, Henry VI, literary analysis, history, Wars of the Roses, theater, English literature, drama, Renaissance

item.title: Adam Brown – A deep but accessible introduction to general relativity 摘要
item.description:
Adam Brown, leader of Google DeepMind's Blueshift team, returns to explain the core idea of general relativity: Einstein's realization that gravitational and inertial mass are equivalent, leading to curved spacetime. He discusses black holes, including why they prevent perpetual motion and the differing observations of infalling vs. distant observers. The episode also explores AI's potential to rediscover general relativity from scratch, touching on philosophy of science. Brown distills complex physics into accessible insights, covering the coincidence that sparked Einstein's 'happiest thought' and the experimental evidence for black holes.
标签: general relativity, black holes, Einstein, physics, AI, science, podcast, Adam Brown, Google DeepMind, philosophy of science

item.title: Quoting Nilay Patel 摘要
item.description:
Nilay Patel argues that augmented reality glasses require a camera continuously recording everything the user sees, with data sent to the cloud for real-time processing, as no sufficiently powerful and efficient chip can fit in glasses stems. This creates a fundamental privacy invasion, and he suggests the societal trade-offs may be too high, questioning whether such products should be developed at all.
标签: augmented-reality, privacy, ai-ethics, surveillance, cloud-computing, hardware, society, technology-ethics

item.title: Premium: The Hater's Guide To The Memory Crisis 摘要
item.description:
The article argues that AI data center demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and LPDDR5X RAM is driving up costs of consumer electronics. NVIDIA's massive GPU orders consume memory at smartphone-maker scale, straining supply from the Samsung-SK Hynix-Micron triopoly. This has led to price hikes on consoles (Steam Machine, PS5, Xbox, Switch 2), Apple devices, and Android phones. Memory manufacturers report record profits with gross margins exceeding 80%, while a class-action lawsuit accuses them of price-fixing. The author notes this is not consumer-driven but fueled by tech giants' AI capex spending, calling it a dangerous transfer of wealth to memory makers.
标签: AI, memory, HBM, DRAM, NVIDIA, price fixing, consumer electronics, supply chain, oligopoly, tech industry

item.title: Apple Sues OpenAI, io, and Former Employees, Alleging Theft of Trade Secrets 摘要
item.description:
Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, io Products, and two former employees—Tang Tan (former VP of product design at Apple, now at Jony Ive's io) and Chang Liu (former senior electrical engineer, now at OpenAI)—alleging theft of trade secrets related to consumer hardware. The complaint describes a coordinated effort to exploit Apple's confidential information, including a specific metal-finishing technique. Notably, Apple avoids naming Jony Ive or Evans Hankey directly, though they are involved with io. Apple states the lawsuit is separate from its ChatGPT integration agreement with OpenAI.
标签: Apple, OpenAI, lawsuit, trade secrets, hardware, Jony Ive, io Products, former employees, consumer hardware, legal

item.title: Building intuition about LLM parameter counts 摘要
item.description:
The author describes their surprise at discovering that token embeddings and the output head account for nearly half of GPT-2 Small's 163M parameters, while attention layers are often overestimated. They built a GPT-2 in JAX and created a visualizer showing parameter breakdowns across embeddings, attention, FFNs, and output head for different model sizes. The tool allows adjusting weight tying and QKV bias, and demonstrates how large vocabularies can dominate parameter counts, especially in smaller models.
标签: LLM, GPT-2, parameter count, embeddings, attention, feed-forward network, weight tying, JAX, model architecture, visualization
